semanticCore: Sleeping, slumbering, resting at night, sleeping lightly, or lying down to rest

keyDerivations.meaning: To sleep, slumber, rest at night, or sleep lightly

figurativeOrRareUsages: كَانُوا قَلِيلًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ (51:17): They used to sleep only a little of the night (praising the night-worshippers).
summaryEn: The root refers to sleeping or slumbering at night (Haj'), praising the pious believers who limited their sleep to devote the night to prayer and repentance.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى النوم بالليل والغرور والاضطجاع للراحة والنوم الخفيف (هجع)، واصفاً المتقين بقلة نومهم وليلهم المشحون بالذكر (قليلاً من الليل ما يهجعون).
rawText: تَهَجَّعَ 5 He slept. (1001 Nights, ii. 321.) هَجْعَةٌ † The setting of a star. (Sgh in TA, art. شرط.) See شَرَطٌ. === A light sleep in the first part of the night. (S.)
